observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), with verifiable supporting documentation and working papers, to adjust CIP accounts, related asset accounts, and accumulated depreciation. Submit this JEV to the audit team for review and evaluation.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile the noted discrepancies between their records, ensuring accurate reports and balances that reflect the actual project status.
Remind the Municipal Engineer to expedite LGU inspection requests to source agencies for completed trust fund projects, ensuring complete documentation for prompt turnover.
Henceforth, direct the Municipal Engineer to immediately provide the Municipal Accountant with all necessary documentation such as Certificates of Completion, Inspection Reports, SWA, etc. to facilitate the prompt reclassification of CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts.

We recommended to the management to:
Remind the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recognizing in the books of accounts the receipt and issuance of in-kind donations other than PPE, but record them instead in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM to be prepared and maintained by the General Services Officer (GSO).
Direct the GSO to prepare and submit the required registry/reports in relation to the receipt and issuance of in-kind donations, in the prescribed formats, to ensure full compliance with the reportorial requirements set forth by COA Circular No. 2014-002.
Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose comparative information in the Notes to Financial Statements relative to the quantity of received, issued and balance of donated relief goods.
Henceforth, instruct the GSO to submit the required monthly reports on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations to the Office of Civil Defense (OCD), copy furnished the COA Auditor, and apply the same to any future similar transactions to be submitted on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month until all donations are fully consumed/utilized.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Adhere faithfully to the accrual basis of accounting for insurance and fidelity bond premiums and to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary adjusting entries supported with verifiable working papers, in order to enhance the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
Ensure, henceforth, that all LTO registration payments are correctly debited to the Taxes, Duties, and Licenses account and duly supported with adequate documents.
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the overstatement of Accounts Payable without any valid obligation in relation to Insurance Expense.

We recommended to the management to:
Instruct the Implementation Committee to expedite the development of written guidelines for SB Ordinance No. 026, s. 2014, ensuring a streamlined and standardized billing and review process. These guidelines must be formally adopted and strictly enforced to improve efficiency and accountability.
Direct the Municipal Engineer to immediately finalize and submit the November and December 2024 billings to the Municipal Accountant’s Office and to the lessee to ensure the timely accrual of rent income and collection thereof, respectively. Any further delays should be duly justified and addressed with urgency.
Henceforth, mandate the Municipal Engineer to issue monthly billing proactively, rather than waiting for lessee requests.
Order the Municipal Engineer to establish a system of regular, documented inspections, including usage logs, and condition assessments of the heavy equipment to ensure proper use and timely maintenance, preventing unexpected repairs and revenue interruptions.
Demand the Municipal Engineer to ensure all necessary information in the billing statement are added to the DOR. Also, implement a verifiable timesheet system for the heavy equipment operator, with supervisory oversight and establish review procedures to eliminate calculation errors.
Obtain written confirmation with the lessee whether there is intent to lease the other heavy equipment specified in the MOA. Otherwise, the agreement should be formally revised to accurately reflect the equipment covered, preventing further misinterpretation and ensuring proper contract enforcement.

We recommended to the management to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere religiously with the correct procedure in the disposition of dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2023-008.
Exhaust possible remedies to collect receivables by specifically requiring the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the concerned accountable officers with past due accounts as of December 31, 2024. If the AO fails to settle his/her accountabilities despite demand, the action could either be salary deduction or filing of appropriate cases, if warranted.
Demand the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Schedules of Dormant Accounts (SDA) on a quarterly basis to be submitted to the audit team on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month, indicating in the remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions such as the absence of documents to validate the claim, death of the accountable officer, unknown whereabouts despite diligent efforts to find him/her, insolvency, exhaustion of all possible remedies to collect receivables, and whether there are pending cases in court involving the subject dormant accounts.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to henceforth ensure that the current transactions and those below ten years are properly documented and properly acted upon immediately to avoid being dormant.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the validity of the unidentified beginning balances totaling ₱7.328 million and effect necessary adjusting journal entries, if warranted, to be supported with verifiable working papers and other pertinent documents.
Ensure to disclose comparative information in the Notes to Financial Statements relative the agency’s dormant accounts.

We recommended to the management to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately issue demand letters to concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance given by LGU Talakag. Proper filing and documentation of these demand letters to barangays should be established.
Enforce the Monitoring Team of the LGU to perform regular monitoring procedures to ensure the implementation of the program, projects or activities is in accordance with the Program of Works prepared by the MEO and with the executed MOA, and ensure that complete inspection reports are filed in the office.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the validity of the unidentified beginning balances totaling ₱0.263 million and effect necessary adjusting journal entries, if warranted, to be supported with verifiable working papers and other pertinent documents.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as recipients of funds transferred from LGU Talakag. Emphasize that barangays with unliquidated balances are ineligible for additional funding until prior grants are liquidated.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and her subordinates to immediately submit all outstanding Disbursement Vouchers, Liquidation Reports, General Journal accounts (including all Journal Entry Vouchers), in compliance with applicable rules and regulations to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance and modification of auditor’s opinion on financial statements due to audit scope limitation. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete supporting documents are completely forwarded to COA for post-audit and verification.
Implement adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines, such as but not limited to expediting the filling in of any necessary vacant positions or create additional position/s, if warranted,  in the MAccO to distribute heavy workload.

We recommended to the management to:
Provide written justifications that the team building cannot be addressed by a more economical mode of CapDev intervention locally, and that it directly addresses the LGU’s actual CapDev needs, as required by Section 3.2 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 02. Additionally, it must be clearly demonstrated that the activity is a publicly recognized good practice that justifies being held outside the geographical island and has a high potential for replication in LGU Talakag, in line with DILG MC 2011-59. Otherwise, refund the amount of ₱5,593,220.00 representing travel claims which may be unnecessary. Henceforth, adhere strictly to DILG MC 2011-59 by refraining from the conduct of team building activity outside Mindanao.
Provide written justifications about the three-night room accommodation in a five-star hotel amounting ₱952,800.00 as the most economical and efficient in the conduct of its team building activity. The amount of ₱444,600.00 representing excess claims beyond the Daily Travel Expenses (DTE) component for accommodation is the same for refund.
Observe austerity measures for the conduct of team building activities to maximize its limited public funds.

We recommended to the management to:
Obtain and submit to the audit team the explicit authorization from DILG for the conduct of said benchmarking activities; otherwise, refund the total disbursed amount.
Ensure that “lakbay aral” activities meet the conditions required by DILG-DBM JMC No. 2 dated September 23, 2016. Remind the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and all approving signatories of Disbursement Voucher to ensure that benchmarking activities are specifically defined or disclosed in the LGU’s CapDev program, ensuring such activities are responsive to the capacity development of participants;
Provide written valid justification that the chosen hotel was the most economical and efficient in the conduct of their activity;
Observe austerity measures for the conduct of “lakbay aral” activities, especially on actual expenses incurred and provide written justifications about the following:
High amount of meal expenses incurred by the LCE despite the meal component of travel allowance were already claimed by other delegates.
Actual departure from Laguindingan Airport on August 15, 2024 at 5:10 PM despite the approved travel order and narrative report indicating an August 14, 2024, arrival in Manila.
BIR-printed invoices lacking seller's information such as registered name, tax information number, business style, and address.
One-night room accommodation in a five-star hotel amounting ₱16,500.00 despite already staying in a three-star hotel with the rest of the delegation for four nights.

We recommended to the management to:
Submit immediately the relevant lacking documents on foreign travel expenses incurred to facilitate our further audit action. Secure documents from respective leagues/associations regarding the breakdown of paid registration fees to accurately account the same, including the pertinent documents necessary for the proper liquidation/accounting thereof. Non-submission by management may warrant issuance of audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be; and,
Strictly observe prudence and judiciousness in the utilization of public funds.

We recommended to the management to:
Stop the improper practice of granting honoraria to the BAC members without adhering to the prescribed maximum honorarium rates per successfully completed procurement; and
Submit immediately to the audit team the noted lacking NOA to substantiate the disbursed transaction; otherwise, re-computation of the correct BAC honorarium will be based on the actual documents attached to the claim. Any excess or overclaim thereat will be subjected to refund or issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended to the management to submit immediately to the audit team the noted lacking documents to substantiate the disbursed transactions. Henceforth, ensured that complete supporting documents are attached as prescribed under the Memorandum of Agreement, DOH AO No. 2022-001, and DOH-DBM JAO No. 2023-001.

We recommended to the management through the BAC to prepare and submit the required APPs, semestral PMR and APCPI to the GPPB in the prescribed formats within the prescribed period to ensure full compliance with the reportorial requirements set forth under Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184 and applicable GPPB circulars.

We recommended to the management to remind henceforth the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare reliable SCBAA and SAAOB reports by including as part of obligations the already transferred unspent appropriations for LDRRMF- MOOE which have been treated already as a trust liability in the TF’s books of accounts at year-end.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to revisit COA Circular 2012-002 and review its pertinent provisions particularly on the Illustrative Accounting Entries for LDRRMF. Further, the Municipal Accountant to set up the required subsidiary ledger accounts under the “Trust Liabilities- DRRMF” account pursuant to Section 5.1.12 of the same circular.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV), supported with verifiable documents and working papers, in order to properly reclassify such transactions lumped to liability accounts and submit immediately to the audit team for verification and post-audit.

We further recommended to the management that the accumulated amount of ₱2,820,500.20 as of December 2023 must be reverted to unappropriated surplus or as one of the sources of funds for a supplemental appropriation of the LGU.

Henceforth, general income should not be temporarily kept under a liability account without bonafide obligations.
observations.reason: Lacking submission of JEV that properly reclassifies the proceeds from liquidated damages and bank interest income from the Other Payables account under Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:

Instruct the Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer to coordinate in expediting the transfer of acquired real properties in favor to the name of the municipality by securing the TCTs pursuant to COA Circular 92-386 to avoid possible land disputes in the future.

Remind the designated General Services Officer to take custody of TCTs and other related documents.

Require the Municipal Accountant to undertake an in-depth analysis and reconciliation of the Land account to enhance the completeness and accuracy of the said affected account and to provide a separate relevant disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements as required by IPSAS 17 that would be beneficial in the decision-making of the users of the financial statements.

The relevant offices responsible for determining the existence of land properties, as well as those involved in the titling, record-keeping, and recording processes, should collaborate closely and reconcile their records.
observations.reason:
Ongoing processing of pre-patent application in DENR for all of the 48 parcels of land not supported with Transfer Certificates of Title (TCTs) under the name of the LGU-Talakag but mostly by Certificates of Tax Declaration (CTDs). The transfer of acquired real properties to the Local Government Unit (LGU) has not yet been finalized.

Additionally, Certificates of Tax Declarations (CTDs) and related documents remain in the custody of the Municipal Treasurer's Office (MTO) instead of the General Services Office (GSO). Moreover, the inventory of land accounts is still pending.

Management has not yet completed the inspection, appraisal and disposal of the identified unserviceable heavy equipment and other motor vehicles. 

In addition, the complete documentary requirements prior inspection by the COA Regional Office No. 10’s Regional Technical Audit Group (RTAG) was not yet submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the Inventory Committee, the GSO, and the Municipal Accountant to continue its conduct of physical count each year and to exert extra efforts in reconciling the reports per physical inventory, per GSO, and per accounting records. Likewise, the management to avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

Further, we recommended to the management to establish good practices on supply and property management by extending efforts to update and maintain complete records for supply and property accountabilities, such as Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR), Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS), property tag, and property cards per category of PPE.
observations.reason: Although management started to update its records for supply and property accountabilities, such as Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR), Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS), property tag, and property cards per category of PPE, management is yet to avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant and the bookkeepers to revisit the Revised Chart of Accounts applicable to LGUs pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 and to prospectively ensure the proper recording of expenses in the books of account so as not to bloat the Other MOE account which will result in the faithful representation of affected account balances reflected in the financial statements.

Moreover, we recommended that for the next budget preparation, the management, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, to allot specific appropriations for the above-cited recurring transactions so as not to unduly charge them against Other MOE account for lack of sufficient budget. Further, streamline the local budget accounts consistent with the said Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended to the management to immediately submit its valid justification/comment on the legality of the disbursed transactions to the COA audit team for our further review and appropriate audit actions. Moreover, the management to exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds consistent with the existing laws, rules, regulations, and Supreme Court jurisprudence.
observations.reason: Management did not submit a written justification on the legality of the disbursed transactions to the COA audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to stop the improper practice of paying and hiring legal advisers bereft of legal basis. Instead, subject to availability of funds, create position for a Municipal Legal Officer to do such functions as mandated by the Local Government Code of 1991. Otherwise, the management may secure the free services of the Provincial Legal Officer and the Provincial Prosecutor.

Moreover, the management may consider granting monthly honorarium to the said Presiding Judge, subject to availability of funds, as prescribed by DBM Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1 dated March 23, 1998 as clarificatory guidelines for item 5.0 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 62 implementing Section Nos. 447 (a)(I)(XI) of RA 7160.

We recommended to the management to submit immediately the aforementioned lacking documents for our further review and audit action. Moreover, the management is reminded to diligently adhere, henceforth, with the necessary documentation requirements and proper computation for hazard pay and subsistence allowance claims. Additionally, the concerned offices should exercise caution in computing the actual benefits rightfully due to them to avoid undue payments.
observations.reason: The lacking documents were not provided by management to the audit team for further review and audit action.
Despite discontinuing the improper procurement of cell cards, management has not yet furnished the audit team with a copy of the new internal guidelines/policy on communication expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to stop the improper granting of representation expenses to the members of the Liga ng mga Punong Barangay which is bereft of legal basis.

We recommended to the management to adhere with the correct practice of obligating and paying the RATA of OICs thru reimbursement basis pursuant to the provisions of DBM LBC No. 103 dated May 15, 2013. Moreover, submit immediately to the audit team the lacking supporting documents as required by COA Circular 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 2023-004.

We recommended to the management to require the concerned offices to fast-track its reconciliation as to the correct amount of unutilized BGCM funds to be reverted to the National Treasury, as required by Section 5.0 of DBM Circular Letter 2021-13 dated December 31, 2021. Afterwards, adhere strictly on the posting and reporting requirements prescribed under Section 6.0 thereof.
observations.reason: Despite management’s assertion that the unutilized BGCM funds to be reverted to the National Treasury were obligated in December 2024 and reverted in 2025, evidence of the obligation as of December 31, 2024, was not furnished to the audit team.
Management has not yet developed internal policies or guidelines regarding the hiring, remuneration, attendance, and disciplinary measures for job order workers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to strictly require the BAC Secretariat to furnish all the perfected contracts, along with their related documents, in earliest time possible and to meticulously review the completeness and orderliness of the awarded contracts based on the provided contract review and technical review checklists prior submission to the audit team. Further, submit immediately the enumerated documents and/or valid justifications for some noted observations for our auditorial and legal review.

More so, the management to correct its current improper practice by directing the BAC to primarily prepare and transmit contract documents to the audit team in lieu of the Municipal Engineer.

Finally, the management to extend utmost priority and attention to address the concerns of the BAC Office vested with crucial functions for a more efficient and effective delivery of government service.
observations.reason: Management is continually retrieving the lacking documents. No justifications were submitted for some noted observations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), supported with verifiable documents and working papers, in order to record/adjust those valid reconciling items reflected in the BRS, especially before closing its books of accounts at year-end. Further, the Municipal Treasurer should monitor all issued checks and if found already staled, the Municipal Accountant to prepare appropriate adjusting entries to correct the same. Moreover, we recommended the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the unrecorded check disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents which the corresponding checks have already been cleared per bank statements. Non-submission thereof to the audit team may warrant issuance of audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be.

Specifically, proper coordination between the Municipal Engineering Office and the Municipal Accounting Office should be made in the processing of these claims. The latter office should meticulously review and ensure that these pertinent documents are completely attached to DVs prior disbursement. 

Moreover, submit immediately to the audit team the lacking supporting documents and valid justification/s on other noted observations for our further review and evaluation.

We recommended to the management the following:

1) Issue demand letters to all accountable officers whether they are still connected or separated from the agency. In cases where the individual is already separated from the agency, demand letters should be sent to their current affiliated agencies or their last known residence via registered mail with return card.
2) Gather and submit the death certificates of the deceased accountable officers together with all other documentation to support the provision of an Allowance for Impairment Loss.
3) As for the outstanding cash advance on Confidential Fund, the management should follow up with the COA- ICFAO in the Central Office as to the status of its liquidation.
4) For receivables which remained outstanding for 10 years or more, filing for requests for write-off supported by necessary documentation (see COA Circular 2016-005 for submission to COA should be initiated.
5) Finally, if sufficient evidence as to the impairment of the receivables is obtained after exhausting all remedies, provision of impairment loss should be facilitated.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to correct the SCBAA by presenting the required balances of continuing appropriations. Submit immediately the signed financial statement to the Office of the Auditor for our review and examination.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), supported with verifiable documents and working papers, in order to record/adjust those valid 
reconciling items reflected in the BRS, especially before closing its books of accounts at year-end. Further, the Municipal Treasurer should monitor all issued checks and if found already staled, the Municipal Accountant to prepare appropriate adjusting entries to correct the same.

Moreover, we recommended the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the unrecorded check disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents which the corresponding checks have already been cleared per bank statements. Non-submission thereof to the audit team may warrant issuance of audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be.
observations.reason: Awaiting for some lacking already encashed DVs still for submission by MTO to the Municipal Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to closely coordinate together to fast-track the in-depth analysis and reconciliation of the PPSB Time Deposit account with doubtful existence.
observations.reason: The PPSB Time Deposit branch is already non-operating. Meanwhile, the management is still on the process of finding key personnel to whom to inquire the status of their time deposit account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management, through the Municipal Accountant, to immediately prepare and submit to the audit team the required CY 2022 financial reports, supported with schedules, for 20% LDF as a special account under the general fund.

We recommended to the management to be prudent and judicious in exercising its fiscal responsibility and accountability over the disbursed funds, by strictly adhering to the proper obligation and disbursement processes. Specifically, the management should ensure that all DVs, payrolls and obligation requests (ObRs) are completely signed by proper authorities in order to delineate full accountability over the disbursed funds. If in case that a representative is authorized to sign on behalf of the proper official, he/she should print his name and full signature to the pertinent document and becomes fully accountable therefor. 

For those disbursements with unsigned DVs, payrolls and ObRs, we recommended management to submit a certification that the concerned authorized official failed to sign the document and is fully responsible and accountable thereof. The details of the disbursed transaction and the nature of his participation thereof should also be specified. Non-submission thereof may warrant issuance of audit suspensions or disallowance, as the case may be.

For those documents signed by an unknown representative, we recommended management to submit a certified list of signature specimens of those authorized/allowed to sign in behalf of the concerned officials. Submit also a joint certification from the proper official and his/her representative that they are fully responsible and accountable to such disbursement. The details of the disbursed transaction and the nature of their participation should also be specified. Non-submission thereof may warrant issuance of audit suspensions or disallowance, as the case may be.

Further, we recommended management to implement a good internal control system to prevent incurrence of such improper disbursement practice with inadequate signatures.
observations.reason: Prospectively implemented by management. The required certifications still to be prepared and  submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to do the following:

• Ensure that the conditions required by DILG-DBM JMC No. 2 dated September 23, 2016 for “lakbay aral” activities;

• Observe austerity measures for the conduct of “lakbay aral” activities, especially on actual expenses not included in the activity design;

• Ensure that proper documentations are attached to each disbursement voucher and that there is a proper disclosure on the transportation claims.

• Provide the official receipts/supporting documents of the following:

* Hotel accommodation and other transportation claims for the liquidation of check numbers 81886625 and 81886697 on November 17-22, 2022.
* Hotel accommodation and plane fare claims of the Vice Mayor for the benchmarking activity on November 28-December 2, 2022.

• Submit valid justifications for the following:

* For the use of official receipts from Philippine Diamond Hotel with OR # 0150165 and 015166 dated December 1, 2022 as supporting documents for the hotel accommodation claims during the November 17-22, 2022 activity. And also, for the use of the same OR# 015166 for two different benchmarking activities. 
* For the payments made without the detailed breakdown or proper disclosure of the routes taken as evidenced by official receipts from Ramiga Travel & Tours and Excelsior Tours & Transport during the travels made on November 17-22, 2022 and November 28-December 2, 2022.
observations.reason: The management failed to substantially comply with all the recommendations but pleaded for the prospective application of said recommendations, if warranted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to ensure that the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) on educational assistance are sufficiently and properly supported with the required documents. Moreover, establish clear-cut internal guidelines thru an enacted ordinance on the proper selection, approval, release, and documentation procedures of such grant to those well-deserved constituents.
observations.reason: Prospectively implemented as to the required documents. Guidelines was already proposed to SB, however, ordinance still has to be enacted by the latter.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to be prudent and judicious in exercising its fiscal responsibility and accountability over the disbursed funds, by strictly adhering to the proper obligation and disbursement processes. Specifically, the management should ensure that all DVs, payrolls and obligation requests (ObRs) are completely signed by proper authorities in order to delineate full accountability over the disbursed funds. If in case that a representative is authorized to sign on behalf of the proper official, he/she should print his name and full signature to the pertinent document and becomes fully accountable therefor.

For those disbursements with unsigned DVs, payrolls and ObRs, we recommended management to submit a certification that the concerned authorized official failed to sign the document and is fully responsible and accountable thereof. The details of the disbursed transaction and the nature of his participation thereof should also be specified. Non-submission thereof may warrant issuance of audit suspensions or disallowance, as the case may be.

For those documents signed by an unknown representative, we recommended management to submit a certified list of signature specimens of those authorized/allowed to sign in behalf of the concerned officials. Submit also a joint certification from the proper official and his/her representative that they are fully responsible and accountable to such disbursement. The details of the disbursed transaction and the nature of their participation should also be specified. Non-submission thereof may warrant issuance of audit suspensions or disallowance, as the case may be.

Further, we recommended management to implement a good internal control system to prevent incurrence of such improper disbursement practice with inadequate signatures.

We further recommended for the submission of the lacking documentary requirements (Office Order, Minutes of BAC Meeting, Notice of Award to the winning bidder, and Certification that the procurement involves competitive bidding) for the paid BAC honoraria enumerated on the above table for us to complete our post audit.

Strictly grant PRAISE Incentives following the existing guidelines, from the establishment of a feasible system to the nomination and evaluation process and up until the actual grant of the award; and
Prohibit en masse grant of PRAISE incentives, particularly monetary incentives, to employees and officers unless the benefit was due on the personal efforts (innovative ideas, inventions, superior accomplishments and other exemplary effort) which directly resulted in monetary savings which shall not exceed 20% of the savings generated.
Ensure that the PRAISE incentives are sourced out from the HRD funds.

We recommended to the management to submit the lacking documents (Authorization Letters for substituted or consolidated receipt of claims, Copies of Appointments/Contracts of Services that are still effective as of December 15, 2021, and Certified list of eligible JOs with their corresponding actual dates of start of work by the HRMO).
Also, we recommended to the management to submit the basis for the computation of rates arrived at since they do not follow the maximum prescribed rates in Administrative Order No. 46.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the identified lacking documents relating to the implementation of the TUPAD Project in order to substantiate the disbursements made.

We recommended to the management to:
Submit a legal basis for the grant of the P10,058,100, allegedly as COVID-19 assistance, to all its Officials and regular employees, job orders, DOH-HRH and other personnel not employees of the LGU-Talakag;
Upon submission or presentation of the legal basis of the grant, please submit the following documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification of the validity and propriety of the claim:
the appointments/contracts of services of regular employees, Job Orders, and other personnel “who are still in actual service as of December 15, 2021”, the certified list of eligible JOs/Cos and regular employees from the HRMO, Certifications for the replacements, and Authorization Letters for substituted receipts of claims.
Justify the selective grant of COVID-19 Assistance that includes even those not considered as low-income households based on actual basic monthly pay.
Explain the use of RA 11469 or the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act which had already expired in June 2020.
We further recommended to the management that thereafter all special purpose/time-bound activities are debited under the “Advances to Special Disbursing Officer” Account.

We recommended to the management to be more cautious in its paper works and to make sure that the actual Purchase Request, Purchase Order, Delivery Receipts, Invoices, Collection Receipts, and other relevant documents would all collaborate with one another.

We recommended to the management to:
Submit the necessary documents (Program designs/needs assessment reports, certified list of beneficiaries, and detailed distribution sheets);
Submit detailed reports which include brands, size, name, quantity of items purchased totaling ₱7,355,222 to enable the tie up of the Purchase Order, Delivery Receipts, Acceptance and Inspection Reports, and the Distribution Sheets.
Thereafter, we recommended to the management to ensure that the journal entry vouchers are supported by complete and irrefutable documents and to accomplish purchase orders, inspection and acceptance reports, and requisition and issue slips with detailed item specification such as the size/volume, brands etc.

We recommended to the management to submit the lacking documents noted in the remarks column of each disbursement.
Thereafter, we also recommended to the management to ensure that all disbursements of government funds should be complete with all the relevant documents, particularly those prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended to the management that the disbursements/payments made for the completed and on-going projects shall be transferred to the proper asset account through the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher with supporting working papers.

We recommended the management through the Municipal Accountant to stop recording inexistent asset and unestablished liabilities in the books of the accounts in order not to misstate the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended to the management to ensure that proper accounting treatment is adapted to financial transactions, particularly purchases of goods, so as not to put the government at a disadvantage and, at the same time, empower the inventory control mechanism of the LGU.

We recommended to the management:

1) Submit the Tax Clearance from the BIR or copy of the Certificate Authorizing Registration (CAR) and Tax Clearance from the Municipal Treasurer in relation to purchase land properties;
2) Transfer the ownership in the Tax Declarations in favor of the Municipal Government; and
3) Process the titling of the land properties.

We recommended to the management to:

1. Strictly grant PRAISE Incentives following the existing guidelines, from the establishment of a feasible system to the nomination and evaluation process and up until the actual grant of the award; and

2. Prohibit en masse grant of PRAISE incentives, particularly monetary incentives, to employees and officers unless the benefit was due on the personal efforts (innovative ideas, inventions, superior accomplishments and other exemplary effort) which directly resulted in monetary savings which shall not exceed 20% of the savings generated.

We further recommended to the management to submit the following documents for evaluation:

1. Nomination Forms of recipients or any document or report which shows the exemplary personal efforts made by each recipient of the incentive;
2. CSC-approved PRAISE; and
3. Detailed computation of the attribution of the savings realigned for the PRAISE incentive in relation to the personal efforts of each recipient.

We recommended to the management to implement projects in accordance to the work description and cost allocation indicated in the approved program of works. Any payments not in conformity with the POW should be supported with a revised POW with justifications stipulating the cost revisions. 

Further, ensure proper monitoring of the disbursement of the projects in order not to exceed its total project cost.
We recommended to the management to carefully plan the mode of implementation for all its infrastructure projects in order to avoid delay implementation or late completion of projects.

We recommended to the management to use Disbursement Vouchers in all ADA disbursements in order to ensure that all payments of the LGU were approved in accordance with the procedural requirements found under Section 344 and 374 of RA 7160.

We recommended to the management to exercise due prudence in recommending approval for the grant of monetization by ensuring that the valid supporting documents and justification for monetization are in accordance with Section 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave.

We recommended to the management to rectify, justify, submit documents to substantiate all the deficiencies noted. 

Emphasis on the submission of the ‘cleaned’ and Certified List from the DSWD is enjoined in order to facilitate the completion of the audit of the SAP grants.

All of the above must be submitted to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise, refund those amounts that cannot be justified, rectified and substantiated with documents. A certified true copy of the Official Receipts for the refunds must be furnished to the Office of the Auditor, if warranted.

We recommended to the management through the BAC Secretariat to regularly check the webpage of GPPB for new issuances (Circulars Resolutions and etc.) in order to keep the BAC updated on the latest issuances and amendments relating to the procurement process. Moreover, we recommend the BAC to comply with all of the requirements of the GPPB.

We recommended the management to submit approved Extensions of Contract Time of L&L Construction should the circumstance warrant issuance thereof. Otherwise, computation of  the liquidated damages should be fairly adapted. Lastly, strict monitoring of the Municipal Engineer as to the rework of the Contractor is encouraged to ensure the safety of the project stakeholders.

We recommended the Municipal Inventory Committee or the  General Services Office to expedite the complete conduct physical inventory taking and make reconciliation on all the Agency’s Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) so as to establish the reliability of balances in the books of accounts of the Municipality. Submission of the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RCPPE.  with appropriate remarks on the condition and whereabouts of the PPE)  to the Accounting, GSO and COA  local auditors not later than January 31 of each year, is also advised.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor must instruct both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to coordinate with each other in the turn-over of those completed municipality-financed and completed barangay facilities to the respective beneficiaries.  After which,they must prepare a JEV for the dropping from the books of account the corresponding cost thereof. Likewise, those computer parts, spare parts and furniture to appropriate accounts.

With the foregoing audit observation, we recommended that the municipal management has to clarify with this audit representation the failure to exercise forfeiture proceeding on defaulting Contractor’s by claiming its Performance Security, as well as the  failure to impose the required administrative sanction prescribed under the above-cited provisions of IRR of RA 9184.  Further, we recommended to apply the amount performance security in the surety bond against the unreleased retention money.

We recommended that the municipal management must have to clarify in writing the above-mentioned observation, particularly for allowing the construction of the subject project by Footprint Construction without executing the necessary contract/agreement.  Further clarification is likewise solicited from municipal management regarding the present status of the project and the future action of with respect on the claim for payment by the contractor.  

More further recommended to include the subject structure as among the property of the municipality based on the procedure of acquisition.

In view hereof, we recommended to the municipal management (BAC, GSO, Canvasser, Inspectorate Team and the End User) to consider the following remedial measures:

1.Establish proper time schedule in the preparation and submission of PPMPs by respective municipal offices.

2.Endeavor to come up with an APP that is timely, accurate and useful to the operations.

3.Endeavor to procure as per PRs (by offices) after having been identified the mode of procurement as consolidated in the approved APP.

4.Endeavor to revisit existing policy of conducting separate canvass or price quotation by the designated three offices (End-users, GSO and BAC) by designating one particular responsible canvasser to facilitate expeditious process. 
5.Endeavor to revisit existing policy on inspection of deliveries for potential improvement of procurement operation. As much as possible, limit the End-users’ role to a mere requisitioning party and plainly as party acceptance of the issuances by the General Services Office.

6.Further, we recommended that municipal management must institute effective monitoring measures to eradicate delays particular to those procurement processes with significant time consumed.

We recommended to require the municipal Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer to expedite the completion of those BUB funded projects not only in coping with government expectation, but to provide early services of the suppose projects to the intended beneficiary communities, as well.

We recommended to the municipal management to expedite the completion of those PGPB projects not only in coping with government expectation, but to also provide intended beneficiaries with early services of supposed projects.
We recommended implementation of the above projects so that the purpose for which the funds were granted is achieved, thus benefitting the constituents.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that Management update its records by posting necessary adjusting entries for identified errors, credit memos and debit memos. As for the stale checks, we advised that they coordinate with the payees of the checks to facilitate proper take of stale checks in line with existing rules and regulations. Lastly, for the alleged bank errors, management should communicate their concern with the bank to appropriately address the matter.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Issue demand letters to all accountable officers whether they are still connected or separated from the agency. In cases where the individual is already separated from the agency, demand letters should be sent to their current affiliated agencies or their last known residence via registered mail with return card.
Gather and submit the death certificates of the deceased accountable officers together with all other documentation to support the provision of an Allowance for Impairment Loss.
As for the outstanding cash advance on Confidential Fund, the management should follow up with the COA- ICFAO in the Central Office as to the status of its liquidation.
For receivables which remained outstanding for 10 years or more, filing for requests for write off supported by necessary documentation (see COA Circular 2016-005) for submission to COA should be initiated.
In case of the outstanding fund transfers which remain unliquidated beyond the agreed time of liquidation, review of the Memorandum of Agreement should be done and the Municipality should send follow up letters to implementing agencies and demand immediate liquidation or refund thereof. Succeeding grants for fund transfers should be suspended until prior liquidation of the outstanding receivables consistent with existing rules and regulations.
Finally, if sufficient evidence as to the impairment of the receivables is obtained after exhausting all remedies, provision of impairment loss should be facilitated.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management should direct the Accountant to make proper adjusting entries to correct the erroneous account classification with working papers and supporting documents. Further, adhere diligently on the classification of accounts defined in the Chart of Accounts.

Recommendation
We recommended that the disbursements/payment made for the completed and on-going projects shall be transferred to the proper asset account through the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher with supporting working papers.

Recommendation
We recommended to management to conduct credit confirmation of the above suppliers/creditors and settle obligations if warranted. Otherwise, revert back the accounts payable aged over two or more years to unappropriated surplus supported with the confirmed zero credit balance from suppliers/creditors through preparation of Journal Entry Vouchers.

Recommendation
We recommended the management to:
Reclassify the erroneously classified sub-accounts under the “Other Payable” account through the preparation of Journal Entry Vouchers supported with working papers.
Reconcile those sub-accounts with negative balances.
Also, adhere diligently to the proper account classification prescribed under the Chart of Accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the subsequent benchmarking activities of the management should adhere with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 97-273 dated October 30, 1997; DILG - DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2 dated September 23, 2016; and Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree 1445.

Recommendation:
The management should direct the Municipal Accountant to faithfully adhere on the submission of paid disbursement vouchers, liquidation reports, reports of collection and deposits along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period required by the regulation, so as to facilitate the timely review and examination of the recorded transactions. Also, observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to COA to avoid the sanctions of none submission of report.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the submission of  all the contracts and its supporting documents of the above-listed projects including the necessary documents related to the Projects for termination, namely: (1) Upgrading of Potable Water Supply (Contract Amount ₱24,140,448.00) and (2) Upgrading of Potable Water System Supply Phase II (Contract Amount ₱10,587,152.28).

Recommendation
We recommended to management to expedite the implementation of the project within the target construction period of 240 days based on the Approved Program of Works.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management should coordinate with the contractor to clarify the differences noted in order to concretely establish which among the two contracts should prevail considering the documents submitted during the bidding process.. More importantly, a written declaration identifying which among the two contracts is enforced by both the management and L & L Construction.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management should require the Contractor to extend the validity period of its Performance Security and at the same time address the deficiency of the bond as required in Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184.

Recommendation:
We recommended the management to submit approved Extensions of Contract Time of L&L Construction should the circumstance warrant issuance thereof. Otherwise, computation of  the liquidated damages should be fairly adapted. Lastly, strict monitoring of the Municipal Engineer as to the rework of the Contractor is encouraged to ensure the safety of the project stakeholders.

We recommended the Municipal Inventory Committee or the  General Services Office to expedite the complete conduct physical inventory taking and make reconciliation on all the Agency’s Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) so as to establish the reliability of balances in the books of accounts of the Municipality. Submission of the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RCPPE.  with appropriate remarks on the condition and whereabouts of the PPE)  to the Accounting, GSO and COA  local auditors not later than January 31 of 
each year, is also advised.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended strict compliance with the provisions set forth in the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 in the procurement of catering and hotel accommodation services, especially procurement which amounts require the adoption of public bidding as its mode, in order to adhere to the principles of transparency efficiency and economy in its procurement process.
We further recommended that management, through the Municipal Accountant, ensure that all supporting documents relative to the conduct of public bidding from the planning stage up to the issuance of the Notice to Proceed should be attached to their respective DVs.

We recommended that management, through the BAC Secretariat submit all the purchase orders and their supporting documents, particularly those enumerated in the table above to enable us to conduct a review.
We recommended that management strictly abide to the implementing rules and regulations in processing government transactions particularly in the purchase of land and other real properties.
We recommended implementation of the above projects so that the purpose for which the funds were granted is achieved, thus benefitting the constituents.

We recommended that management strictly enforce the conditions of the contracts especially in the imposition of liquidated damages on its projects which incurred unjustifiable delays; require the Municipal Engineering Office to provide Statement of Time Elapsed and attach any work suspension orders and resume orders, if any to every progress billings to be able to validate the claims made by the contractors.

We recommended that management:
Strictly follow the prescribed rules and regulations and the new rates in the grant of travel allowances to the employees.
Stop the grant of hotel accommodation in excess of the prescribed rate of P400.00/ day.
The amount of claim for taxi fare of P1,500.00 should be scrutinized if such amount was actually expended for the purpose and if RER is used as proof of receipt, the point of origin and the destination should be indicated in order to evaluate the reasonableness of the claim.
Exercise caution in the utilization of funds to prevent the incurrence of irregular and excessive transactions which could be a ground for disallowance in audit.
We recommend that the management submit appropriate legal basis to support the disbursement of PBB for CY2015, or required refund of amounts received.

We reiterated our recommendation to stop the payments of expenses without legal basis to avoid disallowance in audit. We also recommend the inclusion of Clothing Allowance under the Personal Services item in the agency’s Annual Budget in the subsequent years.
We recommend that management prioritize the implementation of the projects funded under the 20% DF in order to deliver optimum results and benefits to its constituents.
We recommended that management create an inventory committee and issue a directive to conduct physical inventory immediately.
